intitule: Exercise 1: Reading Comprehension - Gender Equality Volunteer

enonce.texte:
Read the text below about a volunteer program and answer the questions that follow.

Mariam is a 16-year-old student from Garoua. She is a volunteer with the 'Girls Can Do It' project. The project aims to promote gender equality in schools. Mariam and other volunteers organize workshops to talk about girls' education. They also help girls who want to study science and technology. Mariam says: 'I love this work because I want all girls to have the same chances as boys.'

questions.texte: 1) Read the text and answer the questions.

sousQuestions.texte: a) What is the name of the project Mariam volunteers for?
sousQuestions.reponse: The project is called 'Girls Can Do It'.

sousQuestions.texte: b) What is the aim of the project?
sousQuestions.reponse: The aim is to promote gender equality in schools.

sousQuestions.texte: c) Why does Mariam love this work?
sousQuestions.reponse: She loves this work because she wants all girls to have the same chances as boys.

intitule: Exercise 2: Vocabulary - Key Terms on Gender Equality

questions.texte: 1) Match each word with its correct definition.

sousQuestions.texte: a) Volunteer →
sousQuestions.reponse: A person who works without being paid.

sousQuestions.texte: b) Gender equality →
sousQuestions.reponse: The state where men and women have the same rights and opportunities.

sousQuestions.texte: c) Workshop →
sousQuestions.reponse: A meeting where people learn and discuss a specific topic.

sousQuestions.texte: d) Promotion →
sousQuestions.reponse: Support or encouragement of an activity or idea.

sousQuestions.texte: e) Opportunity →
sousQuestions.reponse: A chance to do something.

intitule: Exercise 3: Grammar - Active and Passive Voice

enonce.texte: Rewrite the following sentences from active to passive voice or vice versa.

sousQuestions.texte: a) The volunteers organize workshops every month. (Passive)
sousQuestions.reponse: Workshops are organized by the volunteers every month.

sousQuestions.texte: b) Many girls are encouraged by the project. (Active)
sousQuestions.reponse: The project encourages many girls.

sousQuestions.texte: c) The association has trained 50 women this year. (Passive)
sousQuestions.reponse: Fifty women have been trained by the association this year.

sousQuestions.texte: d) A speech on gender equality was given by the president. (Active)
sousQuestions.reponse: The president gave a speech on gender equality.

intitule: Exercise 4: Writing - A Volunteer Activity Report

enonce.texte: You are a volunteer working for a gender equality organization. Write a short report about your activities.

questions.texte: 1) Write a report on your volunteer activities.
questions.reponse: The report should include activities, achievements, and future plans.

sousQuestions.texte: a) Describe your volunteer activities.
sousQuestions.reponse: I am a volunteer with the 'Girls Can Do It' project. We visit schools to talk about the importance of girls' education. We also organize workshops on leadership and self-confidence. Last month, we helped 100 girls to apply for scholarships.

sousQuestions.texte: b) Mention the achievements so far.
sousQuestions.reponse: We have successfully enrolled 50 girls in science classes. We have also created a mentorship program with successful women in our community.

sousQuestions.texte: c) State your future plans.
sousQuestions.reponse: Next year, we plan to expand our program to 5 more schools. We also want to organize a conference on women's rights.

enonce.texte:
Read the dialogue and answer the questions.

Anna: Hello, John! I heard you are a volunteer for gender equality. John: Yes, I am. I work with an NGO called 'Equal Future'. Anna: That's wonderful! What do you do exactly? John: We organize awareness campaigns in schools. We also train teachers on gender-sensitive teaching. Anna: Why did you decide to become a volunteer? John: I believe that everyone should have equal opportunities regardless of their gender. Anna: You are right. How can I join? John: You can visit our website and fill out the application form.

questions.texte: 1) Answer the following questions based on the dialogue.

sousQuestions.texte: a) What is the name of the NGO John works with?
sousQuestions.reponse: He works with an NGO called 'Equal Future'.

sousQuestions.texte: b) What activities do John and his colleagues organize?
sousQuestions.reponse: They organize awareness campaigns in schools and train teachers on gender-sensitive teaching.

sousQuestions.texte: c) Why did John decide to become a volunteer?
sousQuestions.reponse: He decided to become a volunteer because he believes that everyone should have equal opportunities regardless of their gender.

sousQuestions.texte: d) What does Anna want to do after the conversation?
sousQuestions.reponse: Anna wants to join the NGO and asks how to apply.

enonce.texte: Write a short speech (150-200 words) to encourage young people to volunteer for gender equality activities.

questions.reponse: The speech should be persuasive, use active and passive voice, and show commitment to gender equality.

sousQuestions.texte: a) Introduce the topic and your message.
sousQuestions.reponse: Good morning everyone! Today, I want to talk about the power of volunteering for gender equality. Gender equality is not just a women's issue; it is a human rights issue that concerns us all.

sousQuestions.texte: b) Give reasons why people should volunteer.
sousQuestions.reponse: When we volunteer, we create change. We can help girls stay in school. We can encourage women to become leaders. We can build a society where everyone has a chance to succeed. Every action counts, no matter how small.

sousQuestions.texte: c) End with a strong call to action.
sousQuestions.reponse: So, I invite you all to join this cause. Together, we can build a fairer and more equal world. Volunteer today for a better tomorrow!
